description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has just received several month-by-month CSV cost reports that a billing pipeline dropped inside a raw-data area.  
Your goal is to build a neat, always-up-to-date “current” view that relies exclusively on symbolic links, **without** copying or moving the original files.

Initial directory tree (already present on disk):

/home/user/finops/  
└── raw_reports  
    ├── year=2023  
    │   ├── cost_report_2023-11.csv  
    │   └── cost_report_2023-12.csv  
    └── year=2024  
        ├── cost_report_2024-01.csv  
        ├── cost_report_2024-02.csv  
        └── cost_report_2024-03.csv  

Task objectives (all steps must be completed):

1. Create a new directory called /home/user/finops/current_reports (if it does not already exist).  
2. Inside current_reports, create **relative** symbolic links for every CSV that belongs to the most-recent year present under raw_reports.  
   • In this data set, the most-recent year is 2024, so links must be made to every file inside /home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2024/.  
   • The relative path of each link’s target **must** start with “../raw_reports/…”. Absolute paths will not be accepted by the grader.  
3. Inside current_reports, create a convenience link named latest_report.csv that points to the chronologically newest monthly file of that same year (here: cost_report_2024-03.csv).  
   • The target for latest_report.csv must be specified **relative to current_reports** (i.e., “cost_report_2024-03.csv”, not an absolute path).  
4. Produce an audit log at /home/user/finops/symlink_creation.log capturing every link you created during this session.  
   • Append one new line per symlink (including latest_report.csv).  
   • Use this exact format for every line (UTC timestamps only, ISO-8601 “Z”-suffix):  
     YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | <full_symlink_path> -> <target_path_exactly_as_given_to_ln>  
   • Example (illustrative):  
     2024-06-09T15:22:08Z | /home/user/finops/current_reports/cost_report_2024-01.csv -> ../raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-01.csv  
   • The log must **only** contain the new lines you generate in this task (do not delete older lines if the file already exists).  
5. Ensure your final structure meets all assertions:  
   a) /home/user/finops/current_reports exists and is a directory.  
   b) It contains exactly four symlinks: three month-level links plus latest_report.csv.  
   c) Every symlink is valid (readable target) and relative as specified.  
   d) symlink_creation.log exists and includes four new lines in the format defined above.

Hints you may find useful (but not mandatory):  
• The `find` command with `-type f` and `-printf` can help select files dynamically.  
• `readlink -f` or `stat -c %N` can be useful to sanity-check results.  
• When adding to the log, `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` produces the correct timestamp.

The automated check will verify:

• Directory /home/user/finops/current_reports exists.  
• Four symbolic links exist inside it, with correct relative targets.  
• latest_report.csv points to the newest monthly CSV.  
• /home/user/finops/symlink_creation.log contains four well-formatted, chronologically ordered lines (timestamps ascending order is not required, but they must be present).

Do **not** move, rename, or delete any source CSV files. Your entire solution must rely purely on symbolic links and the audit log you generate.
truth:
Initial on-disk contents before the agent starts:

Directories:
/home/user/finops/
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2023/
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2024/

Files:
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2023/cost_report_2023-11.csv      (empty file)
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2023/cost_report_2023-12.csv      (empty file)
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-01.csv      (empty file)
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-02.csv      (empty file)
/home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-03.csv      (empty file)

No current_reports directory exists yet.
No symlink_creation.log exists yet.

Expected new artefacts after successful task completion:

Directory:
/home/user/finops/current_reports/

Symlinks inside /home/user/finops/current_reports/ (names and targets):
1. cost_report_2024-01.csv  ->  ../raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-01.csv
2. cost_report_2024-02.csv  ->  ../raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-02.csv
3. cost_report_2024-03.csv  ->  ../raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-03.csv
4. latest_report.csv        ->  cost_report_2024-03.csv

Audit log:
/home/user/finops/symlink_creation.log must contain exactly four new lines appended in the strict format:
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | /home/user/finops/current_reports/cost_report_2024-01.csv -> ../raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-01.csv
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | /home/user/finops/current_reports/cost_report_2024-02.csv -> ../raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-02.csv
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | /home/user/finops/current_reports/cost_report_2024-03.csv -> ../raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-03.csv
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ | /home/user/finops/current_reports/latest_report.csv -> cost_report_2024-03.csv

The grader will:
• check existence and type (symlink vs file) of all four entries in current_reports,
• verify that the symlinks are relative and match exactly the targets above,
• verify that latest_report.csv resolves (after following both symlinks) to /home/user/finops/raw_reports/year=2024/cost_report_2024-03.csv,
• confirm that symlink_creation.log exists and contains four lines conforming to the timestamp pattern ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z \| .+ -> .+$,
• ensure each of those lines matches the <link_path> and <target_path> pairs listed above.

Permissions: all newly created directories and symlinks should inherit default user permissions; no root access required.
